sections.paragraph: (a place trodden down, as a threshing floor, from the root בּוּס), pr.n. Jebus, an ancient name of Jerusalem, used in the time of the Canaanites, Jud. 19:10 , 11 Jdg 19:11 1Ch 11:4, 1Ch 11:5. The Gent. noun is יְבוּסִי Jebusite, collect. Jebusites, a Canaanitish nation, who inhabited that city with the neighbouring mountains, conquered by David, still in existence in the time of Ezra, Gen 10:16 15:21 Num 13:29 Jos 15:63 2Sa 5:6 Ezr 9:1. This Gentile noun is sometimes put for the city itself (for עִיר הַיְבוּסִי Jud. 19:11 ); Jos 15:8 18:16 and poetically for Jerusalem, Zec 9:7 like כַּשְׂדִּים for Chaldæa.
